prompt:
Small cleanup on this PR branch: remove the dead/redundant dark-mode dconf block in `home/desktop.nix`.

## Background
`home/desktop.nix` currently has TWO dconf color-scheme settings:
1. `dconf.settings."org/gnome/desktop/interface".color-scheme = "prefer-dark";` — the one that actually works (xdg-desktop-portal-gtk reads this GSettings key and reports dark to portal-aware apps).
2. `dconf.settings."org/freedesktop/appearance" = { color-scheme = 1; };` — DEAD. There is no GSettings schema at that dconf path, so nothing reads it. Verified empirically: writing it left the portal still reporting color-scheme=0 (light) until the gnome interface key was added.

## What to do
- DELETE the entire `dconf.settings."org/freedesktop/appearance" = { color-scheme = 1; };` block from `home/desktop.nix`.
- Keep `dconf.settings."org/gnome/desktop/interface".color-scheme = "prefer-dark";`.
- Fix up the surrounding comment: it currently explains the freedesktop block is 'kept as a harmless hint' and contrasts the two. Since the freedesktop block is being removed, trim/rewrite that comment so it no longer references a block that no longer exists — a short note is enough, e.g. that `org/gnome/desktop/interface color-scheme` is the GSettings key xdg-desktop-portal-gtk reads and maps to the freedesktop appearance color-scheme (dark) exposed to apps. Don't leave a dangling reference to the deleted block.
- Do not touch anything else (Zed config now lives in home/zed.nix; leave it alone).

## Acceptance
- `home/desktop.nix` contains only the `org/gnome/desktop/interface` color-scheme setting; the `org/freedesktop/appearance` block is gone and no comment references it.
- Config still evaluates: run `nix flake check` or build the relevant homeConfiguration target and fix any errors before pushing.
- Run `nix fmt`.
- Follow CLAUDE.md: no Co-Authored-By, no mention of Claude/AI/LLM in commit message or PR text. Commit message e.g. 'home/desktop.nix: drop redundant freedesktop appearance dconf key'.

Push to this existing PR branch (#595).
